note: Reverses the fix for CVE-2026-34070 (HIGH), the path-traversal in langchain-core's legacy load_prompt loaders. The fix added _validate_path(), which rejects absolute paths and '..' components before opening a prompt/template file; this suite removes that guard from the primary template-loading path, so a user-influenced prompt config can read arbitrary host files. Diff-visible: the reviewer should see a path-traversal guard deleted immediately before .resolve()/.read_text().

bugs.owasp: A01: Broken Access Control / A05: Security Misconfiguration (CWE-22)
bugs.severity_note: Advisory rates CVE-2026-34070 HIGH (arbitrary host-file read via load_prompt); mapped to the board's 'high', not inflated.
bugs.fixed_by: https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchain commit 27add913474e01e33bededf4096151130ba0d47c (CVE-2026-34070 / GHSA-qh6h-p6c9-ff54, fixed in langchain-core 1.2.22)
bugs.description: Reverts the fix for CVE-2026-34070. In _load_template, the `if not allow_dangerous_paths: _validate_path(template_path)` guard is deleted, so the path popped from a (potentially user-influenced) prompt config is passed straight to Path.resolve() and read_text() with no absolute-path or '..' traversal check. An application that calls load_prompt() / load_prompt_from_config() on attacker-controlled config can then read arbitrary files on the host (constrained only by the .txt suffix check), the classic directory-traversal arbitrary-file-read. The real fix added _validate_path(); reverting removes it from the template path. The reviewer must flag that deleting the traversal guard before a file open reintroduces a path-traversal / arbitrary-file-read vulnerability.

injection.find:
template_path = Path(config.pop(f"{var_name}_path"))
        if not allow_dangerous_paths:
            _validate_path(template_path)
        # Resolve symlinks before checking the suffix so that a symlink named
injection.replace_with:
template_path = Path(config.pop(f"{var_name}_path"))
        # Resolve symlinks before checking the suffix so that a symlink named
